scope: both modules: jvm/ducklake-catalog + jvm/trino-ducklake (src + test + testFixtures)
summary: Reconciled the four per-slice kits. Across 239 ported .kt files, every helper introduced in the trino-main IdiomKit failed the >=3-actual-site bar. Deleted IdiomKit.kt; the unified kit is 'use inline Kotlin idioms; rely on the project-local helper at the one remaining first-line site'. No call-site edits were applied (no surviving helpers to roll out). Verified both suites green.

sites: JdbcDucklakeCatalog.kt:2303 — val message = sqlException.message ?: return false
QuackDuckDbExecutor.kt:366-368 — private fun firstLineOrFull(message: String): String

lowercaseRoot.rationale: 17 actual sites all already write lowercase(Locale.ROOT) directly. Both per-slice kits (trino-main, catalog-main) explicitly noted 'direct call is idiomatic enough' (same length, more familiar to Kotlin readers, no import to manage in IdiomKit's package). Zero callers ever migrated to .lowercaseRoot(). Speculative helper.

safeMessage.rationale: Only 3 null-safe message sites exist. Of those, 2 are in trino-main and are subsumed by first-line logic (so they would call firstLineOf, not safeMessage). The third (catalog-main) uses `?: return false` flow control — would not fit safeMessage's (e, fallback) signature anyway. Zero independent demand.

firstLine.rationale: Only 2 actual sites — InProcessDuckDbExecutor:276 (inline) and QuackDuckDbExecutor:366 (private firstLineOrFull). Below the 3-site bar. QuackDuckDbExecutor's project-local helper is fine as-is; promoting it to IdiomKit would add an import + cross-package reach for 1 caller. The per-slice trino-main kit explicitly said 'future PRs can collapse them' — at unified-kit time, that future has not arrived.

firstLineOf.rationale: Composition convenience for safeMessage+firstLine. With both ingredients dropped, this falls automatically. Zero call sites.

actions_taken.reason: All four helpers failed the >=3-site bar. The per-slice kit-notes.json next to the deleted file is preserved as the slice's audit trail; it documents the helpers as 'introduced, not yet rolled out' — the unified-kit verdict makes that disposition permanent.
Per-slice kit-notes is the workflow's per-slice audit artifact; it stays. The directory no longer contains executable code.

rollout.cross_module_helpers: none — explicitly avoided per workflow constraint (catalog cannot see trino-ducklake; would require cross-module duplication)

ducklake-catalog.rationale: 1 lowercase(Locale.ROOT) inline; 1 ?: return false null-safe message. Zero helpers needed; cross-module IdiomKit would be duplication.

trino-ducklake.rationale: Inline forms (lowercase(Locale.ROOT), e.message ?: ...) read better than the helper indirections at <=16 sites per pattern. The DuckDB-executor first-line logic is a project-local 3-line private fun — too localised to extract.

final_state.default_for_future_authors: Write Kotlin idioms inline. Reach for a helper only when a pattern appears in >=3 distinct sites AND the helper indirection is shorter or clearer than the inline form. The trino-main kit-notes.json shared_helpers_introduced section is the historical record of the speculative-helper experiment that the combine pass reversed.

verification.expected: ducklake-catalog 108/0/0/0 across 33 suites; trino-ducklake 825/0/0/0 across 70 suites
verification.last_run: 2026-06-02 — BUILD SUCCESSFUL (3m 7s). ducklake-catalog UP-TO-DATE (no source touched); trino-ducklake recompiled cleanly after IdiomKit.kt deletion (zero dangling references). Aggregated from on-disk TEST-*.xml: ducklake-catalog 33/108/0/0/0; trino-ducklake 70/825/0/0/0.
